subject: Product Management
title: The HiPPO vs Data Debate
description: One-minute skill: The HiPPO vs Data Debate.

hook.text: Google's first employee, Craig Silverstein, tested 41 shades of blue for link colors. Larry Page thought it was ridiculous.

buildup.text: The HiPPO (Highest Paid Person's Opinion) drives most product decisions. Experimentation threatens this.

discovery.text: At Amazon, Jeff Bezos says: 'If we can make a decision with data, let's do that.

twist.text: Not every decision should be tested. Strategic bets, brand decisions, and ethical choices require judgment, not just data.

climax.text: The best companies use experiments for tactical decisions and judgment for strategic ones. The art is knowing which is which.

punchline.text: Data for tactics. Judgment for strategy. Experiments for everything testable.

quiz.question: When should product decisions rely on experiments vs judgment?
options: Experiments for tactical decisions; judgment for strategic bets, brand, and ethical choices
Every single decision should be A/B tested
Judgment is always superior to data
